note:
麻省理工学院的一组研究人员已经开发出一种设备，可以监测人们的睡眠姿势，而不必使用摄像头或在他们的身体上粘贴传感器。这是一种壁挂式监视器，研究小组将其命名为BodyCompass，它的工作原理是分析无线电信号从房间里的物体反弹时的情况。正如研究人员解释的那样，可以监测睡眠姿势的设备有很多潜在的用途。例如，它可以用来跟踪帕金森氏症的进展，因为患有这种疾病的人失去了在床上翻身的能力。
为了区分从身体反射的无线电信号和从房间里的随机物体反射的信号，该系统将重点放在从人的胸部和腹部反弹的信号上。换句话说，呼吸时运动的身体部位。然后它将这些信号发送到云，这样BodyCompass系统就可以分析用户的姿势。
研究小组训练了他们创造的神经网络，并通过收集26名受试者200小时的睡眠数据来测试其准确性，这些受试者一开始必须在胸部和腹部佩戴传感器。他们说，在根据一周的数据对该设备进行训练后，它在94%的时间里预测了受试者的正确身体姿势。
将来，BodyCompass可能会与其他设备配对，以刺激睡眠者改变位置，比如智能床垫。当这种情况发生时，如果癫痫患者采取了可能致命的睡眠姿势，该设备可以提醒他们，减少睡眠呼吸暂停事件，并通知护理人员转移有患褥疮风险的静止患者。它还可以帮助其他每个人睡个好觉，因为我们绝对都需要它。
团队成员岳世超将在9月15日的UbiComp 2020大会上介绍该系统，不过你可以在麻省理工学院网站(PDF)上阅读他们的论文。
note_en:
A team of MIT researchers has  developed a device that can monitor people’s  sleep postures without having to use cameras or to stick sensors on their body. It’s a wall-mounted monitor the team dubbed BodyCompass, and it works by analyzing radio signals as they bounce off objects in a room. As the researchers explained, a device that can monitor sleep postures has many potential uses. It could be used to track the progression of Parkinson’s disease, for instance, since people with the condition lose their ability to turn over in bed.
 To differentiate between radio signals bouncing off a body and signals bouncing off random objects in a room, the system focuses on signals that bounce off a person’s chest and belly. In other words, the body parts that move while breathing. It then sends those signals to the cloud, so the BodyCompass system can analyze the user’s posture.
  The team trained their creation’s neural network and tested its accuracy by gathering 200 hours of sleep data from 26 subjects who had to wear sensors on their chest and belly in the beginning. They said that after training the device on a week’s worth of data, it predicted the subject’s correct body posture 94 percent of the time.
 In the future, BodyCompass could be paired with other devices to prod sleepers to change positions, such as smart mattresses. When that happens, the device could alert people with epilepsy if they’ve taken a potentially fatal sleeping position, reduce sleep apnea events and notify caregivers to move immobile patients at risk of developing bedsores. It could also help everyone else get a good night’s sleep, because we definitely all need it.
 Team member Shichao Yue will introduce the system at the UbiComp 2020 conference on September 15th, but you can read their paper on  MIT’s website (PDF).
title: 麻省理工学院睡眠监测器可以使用无线电信号跟踪人们的睡眠位置
title_en: MIT Sleep Monitor Can Track People's Sleeping Positions Using Radio Signals

更新：在从风险资本投资者那里筹集了超过1亿美元的资金仅仅几个月后，防欺诈初创公司NS8周四解雇了数百名员工，此前他们通知他们，该公司正在接受欺诈调查。
在发给员工的一封信中，这家总部位于拉斯维加斯的公司周四表示，在“突发和不可预见的情况”之后，它被迫缩减规模。这封信被当地出版物Vital Vegas在推特上分享。这家创业公司的首席执行官亚当·罗加斯上周突然离开了公司，他向福布斯证实了这一消息。
一位今年加入NS8的软件工程师告诉福布斯，员工在周二的全体员工Zoom会议上被告知，美国证券交易委员会(SEC)正在调查该公司涉嫌欺诈的行为。“我们上了车，被告知我们的财务状况并不像我们想象的那样，会有裁员，”这名员工说，由于未获授权接受媒体采访，该员工拒绝透露姓名。
就在NS8有效关闭的几个月前，该公司刚刚宣布了一轮1.23亿美元的首轮大规模融资，由全球风险投资公司LightSpeed Partners牵头。根据创业跟踪机构PitchBook的数据，这轮融资对该公司的估值超过4亿美元。
LightSpeed在给福布斯的一份声明中表示，它仍在收集所发生事情的事实。“这是一个迅速演变的情况，”这位投资者说。“我们对此消息感到震惊，并已采取措施通知我们的有限责任合伙人。现在做进一步评论还为时过早。
在通过LinkedIn发给福布斯的一封冗长的回复中，罗加斯表示，他辞职是因为家庭和个人原因。他接着声称，NS8的董事会和现任管理层利用“美国证券交易委员会(SEC)于2019年11月开始的一项调查，暗示了一种更阴险的说法……我没有带着公司的钱离开。”
他补充说，当NS8在2019年秋季从宣布的资金中筹集到最初的5000万美元时，这家初创公司几乎没有现金了。罗加斯声称，剩余的资金是通过新投资者进行的第二轮融资筹集的。他说：“该公司每个月消耗400万至600万美元，几个月后就会耗尽资金。”
在他的回应中，罗加斯对他亲手挑选的初创公司总裁蒂芙尼·克利曼(Tiffany Kleemann)没有得到“推动公司向前发展的机会”表示失望。但接受“福布斯”采访的员工克里曼表示，他已被任命为剩余业务的代理首席执行官。
NS8于2016年推出，为小企业提供在线欺诈检测和预防软件。今年6月，Crunchbase News报道称，该公司2019年的年度经常性收入跃升了200%。罗加斯告诉该出版物：“由于全球事件，电子商务的采用速度加快，我们看到需求增加了大约五到六倍。”
在裁员之前，该公司雇佣了近225名员工；福布斯无法确切核实有多少员工被解雇，尽管有消息人士称，有数百名员工受到影响，这意味着现在不会有很多员工留在NS8。除了在拉斯维加斯的总部外，NS8还在迈阿密、阿姆斯特丹、新加坡、澳大利亚墨尔本和加利福尼亚州圣拉蒙设有办事处。
罗加斯之前至少创立或领导过另外三家软件公司。根据他的LinkedIn页面，他还在NextGen Ventures担任风险投资者，直到8月份。罗加斯在职业电视网的页面上不再列出他在NS8的角色。
对于即将离职的员工来说，该公司几乎完全倒闭的消息被证明是毁灭性的。“这只是震惊、恐惧和绝望，”接受“福布斯”采访的软件工程师说。“这不好玩。”
note_en:
Updated: Just months after raising over $100 million from venture capital investors, fraud prevention startup NS8 laid off hundreds of employees on Thursday after informing them it was under investigation for fraud.
  In a letter sent to employees, the Las Vegas-based company said on Thursday that it was forced to downsize after “sudden and unforeseen circumstances.” The letter was  shared on Twitter by local publication Vital Vegas. The startup’s CEO, Adam Rogas, abruptly left the company last week, he confirmed to  Forbes.
 A software engineer who joined NS8 this year told  Forbes that employees were informed during an all-hands Zoom meeting on Tuesday that the Securities and Exchange Commission was investigating the company for alleged fraud. “We got on and were told that our finances were not what we thought they were, and there would be layoffs,” said the employee, who declined to be named because they were not authorized to speak to the press.
  NS8’s effective shut down comes just months after the company had announced a massive $123 million Series A round led by global VC firm Lightspeed Partners. That funding round valued the company at more than $400 million, according to data from startup tracker PitchBook.
 Lightspeed said in a statement to Forbes that it is still gathering facts on what has occurred. “This is a rapidly evolving situation,” the investor said. “We are shocked by the news and have taken steps to inform our LPs. It would be premature to comment further at this time."
 In a lengthy response sent to  Forbes over LinkedIn, Rogas said that he resigned for family and personal reasons. He went on to allege that NS8’s board and current management have used “an SEC investigation that began in November of 2019 to insinuate a more insidious narrative...I did not walk away with the companies [sic] money.”
 He added that when NS8 raised an initial $50 million of the announced funding in the fall of 2019, the startup was nearly out of cash. The remaining funding was raised via a secondary round conducted by the new investors, Rogas claims. “The company was burning [$4 million to $6 million] a month and was going to run out of money in a couple of months,” he said.
  In his response, Rogas expressed dismay that his handpicked president for the startup, Tiffany Kleemann, was not “given a chance to move the company forward.” But Kleemann, the employee who spoke to  Forbes says, has been tapped to serve as acting CEO of what’s left of the business.
 NS8 launched in 2016 to provide online fraud detection and prevention software for small businesses. In June, Crunchbase News  reported that the company had reported a 200% jump in annual recurring revenue for 2019. “We’re seeing increased demand of about five to six times as a result of e-commerce adoption accelerating due to global events,” Rogas told the publication.
 The company employed close to 225 people prior to the layoffs;  Forbes could not verify precisely how many employees were let go, though a source said several hundred were affected, meaning not many employees would now remain at NS8. In addition to its headquarters in Las Vegas, NS8 operated offices in Miami, Amsterdam, Singapore, Melbourne, Australia, and San Ramon, California.
 Rogas had previously founded or led at least three other software companies. According to his LinkedIn page, he also worked as a venture investor at NextGen Ventures until August. Rogas’ page on the professional network no longer lists his role at NS8.
 For outgoing employees, news of the company’s near-total closure has proven devastating. “It was just shock, horror and despair,” says the software engineer who spoke to  Forbes. “It’s not fun.”
title: 来源：NS8，一家旨在保护在线交易的初创公司，在SEC欺诈调查中裁员约240人；CEO上周突然离职
title_en: Source: NS8, a startup designed to protect online transactions, lays off ~240 staff amid SEC fraud investigation; CEO abruptly departed last week

第一级你可以为你的私人活动租用名人。不只是音乐家，还有真正的男女演员。
贝莱尔的超级富豪过去常常在10月下旬为他的孩子举办生日派对，所以他们全力以赴地举办了一个万圣节主题的派对。
每年他们都会聘请一些出色的运动员出现在比赛中；一年是托尼·霍克，另一年是一些奥运会体操金牌获得者。
让我大吃一惊的是，他们雇了黛米·摩尔、安东尼·凯迪斯和贝内西奥·德·托罗作为派对的嘉宾，让他们在聚会上闲逛，假装自己是这个孩子的朋友。
请注意，这是一个儿童万圣节派对，设在户外一个巨大的花园里，散布在网球场和草坪上，有游戏、自助餐、甜点桌子、玉米饼摊、煎蛋卷摊、烧烤、披萨、汉堡等。不能喝酒，任何人都不能进去。所有的活动工作人员都穿着万圣节的服装，非常酷。
但是看到Kedis和Amp；de Toro坐在一起表示同情，这是令人难过的……。你可以看到他们脸上的表情，他妈的，我们为了一张薪水而做的事情。他们当时在一个孩子的聚会上。
第二级，但看到凯迪斯和德托罗坐在一起表示同情，这是令人难过的……。你可以看到他们脸上的表情，他妈的，我们为了一张薪水而做的事情。他们当时在一个孩子的聚会上。
是的，我的心为这两个百万富翁流血，他们可以拿到几千美元，仅仅是为了在一个孩子的派对上成为荣耀的艺人。
前一段时间，这里的一些人谈到了他为亿万富翁担任人事经理的经历，以及他们之间的情况有多么大的不同。
他举的具体例子是，当这些人想去旅行时，事情是如何运作的，并向员工发出任何通知。
发生的情况是，一个高级团队被提前几天派去勘察租/买的位置，并报告衣柜空间、抽屉空间等的确切尺寸。回到家里的人会检查衣服、珠宝等，并起草一份优先列表，然后发送给高级团队。然后，高级团队在接下来的两天里购买物品清单。整个衣柜、珠宝套装、化妆包、洗澡用品等等。他们拿不到的任何东西(没有足够的时间，或者是像富人偶尔佩戴的传家宝手表这样的独一无二的东西)都会转给房屋团队。这个家庭的日程安排是这样的：在旅行当天，当他们离开家的那一刻，整个团队的人就会匆忙赶来，打包所有剩余的物品(只有在家人离开后，你不会想要拒绝他们拿到他们的物品，哪怕只有几秒钟)，然后在家人吃午饭或其他什么东西的时候，这些物品会被提前送到机场。在降落时，他们的行李走一条路线(直接)，家人走一条类似的间接路线(除非另有指示)，这样当他们到达地点时，他们所有的物品都不只是拆包，而是放在适当的有组织的位置，准备好使用，而不会有任何先进的团队被家人看到。
当这家人离开这个地方时会发生什么？相反，同样的情况也是如此，但通常所有回购的物品都是以某种方法处理掉的。如果他们不是快速地连续前往太多不同的地方，那么每次全家去某个地方时再买，即使不是更便宜，也更容易。
第二级这是埃及的一些法老拉屎级别。这些亿万富翁，即使是功能正常的人，在生活中几乎所有的事情都是为他们做的，他们是怎么做的？
第一级这在拉丁美洲是一个众所周知的现象，但我想其他地方也会发生这种现象。
第二级，当然在黑帮中也会发生这样的事情。我认识一个人，他为他的老板服刑8年，还拿到了工资。
我记得在什么地方读到过，一位著名的印度演员酒后驾车，他杀死了两个无家可归的人，后来他让他的司机替他进了监狱。
第一级我在这里看不到，但除了富人，绝大多数金融产品对所有人来说都是遥不可及的。富人变得更加富有的一个原因是，他们可以获得我们从未听说过的投资。你看过“大空头”吗？你认为为什么高盛(Goldman Sachs)花了一周时间才对迈克尔·伯里(Michael Burry)博士的住房空头头寸进行了正确定价？因为他们是在为自己和客户保住这个位置。这些金融工具是如此复杂，对它们的监管又是如此错综复杂，如果高盛真的不做任何非法的事情，比如允许它们在一段时间内对资产进行错误定价，我也不会感到惊讶。可能是在资产定价复杂的幌子下，但实际上，这只是他们获得普通人无法相信的优势的缓冲。
想象一下，去看一场赛马，并且能够在比赛接近尾声时为马下注。有钱人都明白这一点。
一级民营银行。富人使用像大通银行这样的银行，但他们不是通过正规分行开设银行，而是使用大通私人银行业务。他们从不等待银行家拿起电话，他们可以在当天获得存款、信用额度等。将300万美元存入你的支票账户，你就会接到银行私人银行组的电话。
我曾经是一位中层电影制片人的私人助理。我在私人分行为她办理银行业务所花费的时间，与去我的分行领一张本票或诸如此类的东西相比，是微不足道的。还有一位分析师每天会给我打三次电话，和我讨论她的投资组合，然后我会给她两分钟的简报，而她会阅读他们发来的任何电子邮件，她告诉我，她每天三次做四分钟的工作，赚了数百万美元。那狗屎让我发疯了。她还住在布鲁克林DUMBO的一层阁楼里，她拥有的一栋大楼只印钞票，她在90年代买下它，作为房地产投资，当时该地区一贫如洗。财富可以买到获取知识的途径。

level 1  You can rent celebrities for your private events. Not just musicians, but bona fide actors & actresses.
 Super rich guy in Bel Air used to host his kid's birthday party in late October, so they went all out for a Halloween themed party.
  Each year they'd hire some fantastic athlete to appear at the event; 1 year it was Tony Hawk, another year it was some Olympic gold medal gymnastic winners.
 The one that threw me was when they hired Demi Moore, Anthony Kedis & Benecio de Toro to be "guests" at the party, to hang out and pretend they were friends with the kid.
 Mind you this was a KID'S Halloween party, set outside in a huge, massive garden, spread out over tennis courts & lawns, with games, buffets, dessert tables, taco stands, omelette stands, bbq, pizza, burgers, etc... no booze, no one allowed inside. All the event staff were dressed in halloween costumes, it was VERY cool.
 But it was sad to see Kedis & de Toro sitting together commiserating.... you could see the 'fuck, the things we do for a paycheck' look on their faces. They were at a KID'S party ffs.
 level 2  But it was sad to see Kedis & de Toro sitting together commiserating.... you could see the 'fuck, the things we do for a paycheck' look on their faces. They were at a KID'S party ffs.
 Yeah, my heart bleeds for these two millionaires who can command thousands of dollars just to be glorified entertainers at a kid's party.
   level 1  A while back some guy on here was talking about his experience working as a sort of personnel manager for a billionaire and how things are just wildly different for them.
 The specific example he used was how things work when these people want to go on a trip, and give any notice at all to their employees.
 What happens is that an advanced team gets sent ahead by a few days to scope out the rented/bought location and report back exact dimensions for closet space, drawer space, etc. People back at the home go through the clothing, jewelry, etc, and draw up a priority list which is sent to the advanced team. The advanced team then spends the next two days purchasing the list of items. Entire wardrobes, jewelry sets, makeup kits, bathing supplies, etc. Anything they cannot get (not enough time, or is one-of-a-kind like the family heirloom watch the rich dude wears every now and then) is relayed to the house-team. The family's schedule is arranged such that the moment the family leaves the house on the day of travel, a whole team of people rushes through and packs up all the remaining items (only after the family leaves, you wouldn't want to deny them access to their items for even a few seconds) which are then sent ahead to the airport while the family has a lunch or something somewhere. Upon landing, their luggage takes one route (direct) and the family takes a similarly indirect route (unless otherwise directed) such that by the time they get to the location all of their items are not just unpacked but in their proper organized locations and ready for use without any of the advanced team ever being visible to the family.
 What happens when the family leaves the location? The same situation in reverse, but quite frequently all of the repurchased items are just disposed of in some method. It's just easier, if not cheaper, to rebuy them each time the family goes somewhere if they aren't travelling to too many different locations in quick succession.
 level 2  This is some pharaohs of egypt level shit. How are these billionaires even functional people when literally everything in done for them in life.
       level 1  This is a known phenomenon in Latin America but I imagine it happens in other places as well.
 level 2  Certainly amongst the yakuza it happens. I knew a guy who did 8 years for his boss and got PAID.
    level 2  I remember reading somewhere that a famous Indian actor was driving under the influence and he killed 2 homeless people, later on he got his driver to go to jail for him.
   level 1  I don't see it on here, but the vast majority of financial products are out-of-reach for all but the rich. One reason the rich get richer is that they have access to investments that we've never heard of. Ever seen "The Big Short" why do you think Goldman Sachs took a week to correctly price Dr. Michael Burry's housing-short position? Because they were securing that position for themselves and their clients. Those financial instruments are so complicated and the regulation on them so byzantine that it wouldn't surprise me if Goldman actually didn't do anything illegal, like they're allowed, at their discretion, to misprice an asset for a certain period of time. Probably under the guise of the assets being complicated to price, but really it's just a buffer for them to get an edge that regular people couldn't believe.
 Imagine going to a horse race an being able to bet on the horses near the end of the race. Rich people get that.
  level 1  Private banks. Rich people use banks like Chase, but they don't bank through regular branches, instead they use Chase Private Banking. They never wait on hold for a banker to pick up the phone, they get same day access to their deposits, lines of credit, etc.. Deposit $3 million into your checking account and you'll get a call from your Bank's private banking group.
 level 2  I used to be a personal assistant to a mid-level film producer. The amount of time I’d spend doing her banking at the private branches was infinitesimal compared to going to my branch to get a cashier cheque or the like. There was also an analyst who would call me three times a day to discuss her portfolio with me and then I’d give her two minute briefings while she read whatever they emailed over, she told me she made millions doing four minutes worth of work three times a day. That shit was bonkers to me. She also lived in a full floor loft in DUMBO, Brooklyn in a building she owned that only printed money, she bought it in the 90s as a real estate investment when the area was destitute. Wealth buys access to knowledge.
title: 富人会买些穷人甚至不知道可以买到的东西呢？
title_en: What do the rich buy that the poor don't even know is available for purchase?

note:
根据本周提交的一份法庭文件，现已倒闭的血液检测初创公司Theranos的名誉扫地的创始人兼前首席执行官伊丽莎白·霍姆斯(Elizabeth Holmes)可能会利用精神疾病作为对一系列联邦欺诈指控的辩护。
霍姆斯和特里诺斯的前总统拉梅什·巴尔瓦尼于2018年6月被指控犯有9项电信欺诈罪和2项合谋电信欺诈罪。联邦检察官指控这对在被指控的罪行中有恋情的人合谋诈骗Theranos投资者超过1亿美元，并欺骗医生和患者，让他们错误地认为该公司有缺陷的血液测试技术可以可靠地进行准确的健康测试，只需刺一下手指上的血滴。
根据本周提交的法庭文件，福尔摩斯-目前正与巴尔瓦尼分开受审-去年12月通知法院，她计划提交与有罪问题有关的“与精神疾病或缺陷或任何其他精神疾病有关的专家证据”。提供这些证据的专家在文件中被命名为加州州立大学富勒顿分校的心理学家明迪·机械(Mindy Mechanical)。
根据机械学院的网站，她专注于“暴力、创伤和受害的心理社会后果，重点是针对妇女的暴力和其他形式的人际暴力。”该网站还指出，Mechanical“经常在涉及人际暴力的复杂法律案件中提供专家证词。”
法庭文件接着说，具体的精神健康证词将涵盖哪些内容，但信息已经过编辑。
针对福尔摩斯提供精神健康证据的计划，联邦检察官要求他们还应该能够检查福尔摩斯的精神状态，并在法庭上提供自己的精神证据，作为公平的反驳。
该案的法官，加利福尼亚州北区的美国地区法官爱德华·达维拉(Edward Davila)同意检察官的观点。因此，他命令福尔摩斯在连续两天的时间里，由两名政府指定的医生进行长达14小时的心理测试和精神评估。戴维拉还下令将政府对福尔摩斯的评估记录在视频中-不顾福尔摩斯的反对。
在Theranos及其宠儿出身的弃儿漫长而戏剧性的传奇故事中，精神状况辩护只是最新的转折。福尔摩斯是一名大学辍学生，自2003年创立自己的初创公司以来，年仅19岁的她就获得了媒体的热烈关注。她以史蒂夫·乔布斯式的时尚选择和大胆宣称的技术实力而闻名。2014年，该公司估值为90亿美元。
但根据此后的多家媒体调查，Theranos的技术存在缺陷，该公司的许多说法都具有误导性或虚假。在幕后，巴尔瓦尼与福尔摩斯共同领导公司，据报道，他在办公室内被称为“执行者”。
福尔摩斯在2002年夏天认识了巴尔瓦尼，当时他们都在北京大学学习普通话，当时她大约18岁，他37岁。他们后来恋爱了，巴尔瓦尼于2009年加入公司。
霍姆斯的审判目前定于2021年3月进行，巴尔瓦尼的审判预计将紧随其后。他们都面临着长达20年的监禁。
note_en:
Elizabeth Holmes—the disgraced founder and ex-CEO of the  now-defunct blood-testing startup, Theranos—may use a mental condition as a defense against  a slew of federal fraud charges, according to  a court document filed this week.
 Holmes and Theranos’ former president Ramesh “Sunny” Balwani were charged in June 2018 with nine counts of wire fraud and two counts of conspiracy to commit wire fraud. Federal prosecutors allege the pair—who were romantically involved during the alleged crimes—engaged in conspiracy to defraud Theranos investors out of more than $100 million and defraud doctors and patients into falsely believing the company’s faulty blood-testing technology could reliably perform accurate health tests with just drops of blood from a finger-prick.
 According to the court document filed this week, Holmes—who is now being tried separately from Balwani—notified the court last December that she plans to submit “expert evidence relating to a mental disease or defect or any other mental condition” that has bearing on the issue of guilt. The expert providing such evidence was named in the document as psychologist Mindy Mechanic, of California State University, Fullerton.
 According to  Mechanic’s faculty website, she focuses on “psychosocial consequences of violence, trauma, and victimization with an emphasis on violence against women and other forms of interpersonal violence.” The site also notes that Mechanic “frequently provides expert testimony in complex legal cases involving interpersonal violence.”
 The court document goes on to say what the specific mental health testimony will cover, but the information is redacted.
 In response to Holmes’ plans to provide mental health evidence, federal prosecutors requested that they should also be able to examine Holmes’ mental state and provide their own psychiatric evidence in court as a fair rebuttal.
  The judge in the case, US District Judge Edward Davila of the Northern District of California, agreed with the prosecutors. As such, he ordered Holmes to undergo up to 14 hours of psychological testing and psychiatric evaluation by two government-appointed doctors over the course of two consecutive days. Davila also ordered that the government’s evaluation of Holmes be recorded on video—over Holmes’ objections.
 The mental condition defense in just the latest twist in the long, dramatic saga of Theranos and its darling-turned-pariah founder. Holmes, a college dropout, garnered glowing media attention after founding her startup in 2003 at the age of just 19. She was known for her Steve Jobs-like fashion choices and bold claims of technological prowess. In 2014, the company  was valued at $9 billion.
 But according to multiple media investigations since, Theranos’ technology was faulty and much of the company’s claims were misleading or false. Behind the scenes, Balwani was co-leading the company with Holmes and was reportedly known around the office as “the enforcer.”
 Holmes had met Balwani while they both studied Mandarin at Beijing University during the summer of 2002,  when she was about 18 and he was 37. They later became romantically involved and Balwani joined the company in 2009.
 Holmes’ trial is currently scheduled for March 2021 and Balwani’s trial is expected to follow. They both face up to 20 years in prison.
title: Theranos欺诈案法官下令对福尔摩斯进行14小时心理测试
title_en: Judge in Theranos fraud case orders 14-hour psychological test for Holmes

 In addition, Apple is also offering more flexibility to “reader” apps like Netflix, and said it’s supporting a new category called “free stand-alone” apps, which could include email apps like the disputed Hey.

美国国税局宣布悬赏高达62.5万美元给任何能破解莫内罗隐私的人。
美国国税局(US Internal Revenue Service)悬赏高达62.5万美元的赏金，任何人只要能破解Monero等据称无法追踪的隐私硬币，以及比特币闪电网络(Lightning Network)上的跟踪交易，就可以获得高达62.5万美元的赏金。
上周公布的官方提案称，美国国税局将在9月1日之前接受工作原型形式的提交。16.如获接纳，申请人将获得50万元的首期付款。
这笔赠款将允许申请者在接下来的八个月内将他们的原型开发成一个可行的概念。待试验完成并获政府批准后，政府会再批出十二万五千元拨款。
IRS-CI正在寻求与一家或多家承包商合作的解决方案，为追踪和归属隐私币提供创新的解决方案，如专家工具、数据、源代码、算法和软件开发服务。
公告将该倡议的主要目标定义为帮助美国国税局刑事调查(CI)特工追踪交易-包括识别钱包、交易日期和时间-以及转账金额。该机构希望使用这些工具来预测标记地址的未来交易。最终产品还必须提供CI完全控制，能够进一步开发或修改它们，以便组织不必依赖任何外部供应商。
莫内罗是犯罪组织首选的虚拟货币之一，而不是比特币等更具追踪性的加密资产。美国国税局指出，由于“隐私问题”，XMR正被索迪诺基比勒索软件集团用于未来的所有赎金要求和交易。
随着当局提高了密码取证能力，并雇用了Chainanalysis等私人承包商的技能，犯罪集团对隐私硬币的需求有所增长。近年来，Chainanalysis协助执法部门追踪比特币和其他加密货币交易，成功打击虐待儿童、洗钱和恐怖分子融资。上个月，Chainanalysis在摧毁三个恐怖组织中发挥了不可或缺的作用。
隐私硬币是帮助犯罪分子混淆交易的关键策略，美国国税局表示：
目前，用于追踪涉及隐私加密货币硬币(如Monero)的交易或为非法行为提供隐私的其他离链交易的调查资源有限。
区块链分析公司CipherTrace声称拥有一种可以追踪Monero交易的新工具，尽管其能力尚未得到证实。该公司宣布，该工具耗时一年多的开发，将由美国国土安全部使用。CipherTrace首席金融分析师约翰·杰弗里斯(John Jeffries)表示，该工具可以追踪被盗的Monero用于非法交易的源头，比如在勒索软件案件中。
note_en:
The United States Internal Revenue Service has announced a bounty of up to $625,000 to anyone who can crack Monero’s privacy.
   The United States Internal Revenue Service has offered a bounty of up to $625,000 to anyone who can break purportedly untraceable privacy coins such as Monero (XMR) as well as trace transactions on Bitcoin’s ( BTC) Lightning Network.
 The official  proposal, published last week, says the IRS will accept submissions in the form of working prototypes until Sept. 16. If accepted, applicants will receive an initial payment of $500,000.
 This grant will allow applicants to develop their prototype into a working concept over the next eight months. Once the pilot test is completed and approved by the government, a further $125,000 grant will be awarded.
 "IRS-CI is seeking a solution with one or more contractors to provide innovative solutions for tracing and attribution of privacy coins, such as expert tools, data, source code, algorithms, and software development services."
 The announcement defines the initiative’s primary objectives as helping IRS Criminal Investigation, or CI, special agents to trace transactions — including identifying wallets, transaction dates and times — and amounts transferred. The agency hopes to use the tools to predict the future transactions of flagged addresses. The final products must also provide CI full control, with the ability to further develop or modify them so that the organization does not have to rely on any external vendors.
 Monero is one of the virtual currencies preferred among criminal organizations over more traceable crypto assets like Bitcoin. The IRS noted that XMR is being used for all future ransom demands and transactions by ransomware group Sodinokibi due to its “privacy concerns.”
  Demand for privacy coins among criminal syndicates has grown as authorities have increased their crypto forensics capabilities and employ the skills of private contractors such as Chainalysis. In recent years, Chainalysis has assisted law enforcement in tracking Bitcoin and other cryptocurrency transactions to successfully fight child abuse, money laundering and terrorist financing. Last month, Chainalysis was integral in the takedown of three terrorist organizations.
 Privacy coins are a key strategy to help criminals obfuscate their transactions, with the IRS stating:
 "Currently, there are limited investigative resources for tracing transactions involving privacy cryptocurrency coins such as Monero or other off-chain transactions that provide privacy to illicit actors."
 Blockchain analytics firm CipherTrace claims to have a  new tool that can trace Monero transactions, although its capabilities are yet to be confirmed. The company announced that the tool, which took more than a year to develop, will be used by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. CipherTrace chief financial analyst John Jeffries said the tool can track stolen Monero used for illegal transactions back to the source, such as in ransomware cases.
title: 美国国税局悬赏62.5万美元给任何能破解莫内罗和闪电的人
title_en: The IRS offers a $625,000 bounty to anyone who can break Monero and Lightning

note:
这篇报道是苹果活动的一部分，我们全面报道了来自苹果总部的最新消息。苹果正在改变其App Store规则，允许提供游戏流媒体服务，让人们在互联网上玩视频游戏的方式类似于他们在Netflix上流媒体电影的方式。此举标志着这家iPhone制造商承认一些人玩电子游戏的方式正在发生变化。
苹果表示，新规定将允许连接到游戏流媒体服务的应用程序，如索尼的PlayStation Now、微软的Xbox XCloud、谷歌的SStadia和英伟达的GeForce Now。但这里有一个陷阱。
苹果赢得了胜利，而不是像电影和电视公司那样，简单地让游戏公司发布应用程序。取而代之的是，游戏流媒体公司被要求为其服务上的每一款游戏提交一款应用程序，然后苹果将像审查App Store中的其他180万个程序一样对其进行审查。
因此，想要使用游戏流媒体服务的人会在苹果的App Store上找到与之相关的游戏，其评论和评分与其他应用程序一样。当他们打开这款游戏的应用程序时，它很可能会连接到流媒体服务，让他们开始玩。
这也意味着，如果一项订阅服务提供数百款游戏，每款游戏都需要一个单独的应用程序来连接该服务。
苹果在其新的指导方针中表示，流媒体游戏服务订阅中提供的游戏必须直接从App Store下载，设计必须避免订户重复付费，并且不应对非订户客户不利。
苹果公司的这一举措是对人们与娱乐互动方式迅速转变的最新承认。几年前，游戏订阅只是该行业的一小部分，很少有公司提供。但随着旧游戏目录的增加，游戏制造商表示，他们已经意识到有机会帮助人们以新的方式访问旧游戏，包括通过订阅、游戏流媒体或某种组合。
此举也突显了苹果在游戏行业的实力。虽然iPhone和iPad通常不被认为是传统的视频游戏机，但这些设备在175个国家有超过10亿人使用。而游戏是人们在这些设备上做的最受欢迎的事情之一。对于微软、索尼和任天堂等游戏机制造商来说，这使得苹果的设备以及搭载谷歌Android软件的设备成为一个诱人的机会。
这家全球iPhone手机制造商的新规定也将在该公司预计将为其iPhone和iPad发布其iOS14操作系统和iPadOS 14软件之前生效。预计苹果还将在未来几周的某个时候发布一套全新的iPhone，采用新的设计，并承诺提供超高速的5G无线速度。
开发者和科技公司表示，5G承诺的速度使得在移动中进行游戏流媒体成为可能。一些测试，包括最近在韩国进行的一次测试，都被证明是有希望的。
与此同时，苹果改变了规则，这意味着该公司实际上正在扭转早先反对在iPhone和iPad上提供流媒体游戏服务的立场。
苹果公司的App Store审查指南，其员工用来决定提交给App Store的程序是否符合公司关于安全性、可用性和货币化的标准，但此前该指南不允许提供游戏流媒体服务。在此之前，苹果公司的员工用来决定提交给App Store的程序是否符合公司关于安全性、可用性和货币化的标准，但此前该指南不允许提供游戏流媒体服务。今年8月，该公司在一份声明中表示，它担心无法审查给定游戏服务上的每一款游戏内容。该公司的变革使之成为可能。
微软此前批评了苹果限制其Xbox游戏流媒体服务的规定，该公司在一份声明中表示，它不同意苹果的做法。
微软发言人在一份声明中表示，这对客户来说仍然是一种糟糕的体验。游戏玩家想要像对待电影或歌曲一样，直接从一款应用程序内的精选目录直接进入游戏，而不是被迫下载100多个应用程序才能从云端玩单个游戏。我们致力于将游戏玩家放在我们所做的一切事情的中心，提供出色的体验是这一使命的核心。
游戏流媒体服务第一次掀起波澜是在2010年，当时OnLive和Gaikai等服务证明了它们可以在互联网上流媒体游戏。这两家公司最终都被索尼收购，索尼于2014年开始提供PlayStation Now服务。自那以后，它已经成长为提供800多种图书。
最近，微软(Microsoft)、谷歌(Google)和芯片制造商英伟达(Nvidia)都提出了他们对这项技术的看法，这项技术的动力来自订阅或个人游戏购买，或者两者的某种组合。
例如，微软计划通过其每月14.99美元的Xbox Games Pass Ultimate服务提供流媒体技术，该服务让人们可以在Xbox或PC上观看100多款游戏，以及其Xbox Live游戏社交网络。该公司已经在Android设备上首次推出了Xbox流媒体服务，但在8月份，该公司表示，苹果的App Store规则意味着，Xbox流媒体服务不能在iPhone或iPad上运行。
苹果公司当时表示，苹果是唯一一家拒绝消费者提供云游戏服务和Xbox Game Pass等游戏订阅服务的通用平台。目前还不清楚微软最终是否会向苹果的政策让步。
谷歌和英伟达均拒绝置评。索尼没有立即回复记者的置评请求。
尊重他人，保持文明礼貌，不离题。我们删除违反我们政策的评论，我们鼓励您阅读这些评论。我们可以随时关闭讨论线程。

This story is part of  Apple Event, our full coverage of the latest news from Apple headquarters.    Apple is changing its App Store rules to allow game streaming services, which let people play video games over the internet in a way that's similar to how they stream movies on Netflix. The move marks the iPhone maker's acknowledgement of the changing ways some people are playing  video games.
 Apple  said the new rules will allow apps that connect to game streaming services such as  Sony's PlayStation Now,  Microsoft's Xbox xCloud,  Google's Stadia and  Nvidia's  GeForce Now. But there's a catch.
   Apple won't simply let the game companies publish an app like movie and TV companies do. Instead, game streaming firms are required to submit an app for each individual game on their service, which Apple will then review just like any of the other 1.8 million programs in the App Store.
                                             As a result, people who want to use a game streaming service will find its associated games on Apple's App Store, with reviews and ratings like every other app. When they open the game's app, it'll likely then connect to the streaming service and let them begin playing.
 This also means that if a subscription service offers hundreds of games, each one will need a separate app that connects to that service.
 "Games offered in a streaming game service subscription must be downloaded directly from the App Store, must be designed to avoid duplicate payment by a subscriber, and should not disadvantage non-subscriber customers," Apple  said in its new guidelines.
  The company's step is Apple's latest acknowledgement of the way people are rapidly shifting the way they interact with entertainment. Several years ago, game subscriptions were a small part of the industry, offered by few companies. But as back catalogs have grown, game makers say they've realized an opportunity to help people access older games in new ways, including through subscriptions, game streaming or some combination.
 The move also emphasizes Apple's power in the game industry. Though the iPhone and iPad aren't typically considered traditional video  game consoles, the devices are used by more than 1 billion people across 175 countries. And  gaming is  one of the most popular things people do on the devices. That makes Apple's devices, as well as ones powered by Google's Android software, an appealing opportunity for console makers like Microsoft, Sony and  Nintendo.
 The  iPhone maker's new rules also go into effect just before the company is expected to release its  iOS 14 and iPadOS 14 software for its iPhones and  iPads. Apple is also expected to announce a   new set of iPhones sometime in the coming weeks, featuring a new design and promising superfast   5G wireless speeds.
 Developers and tech companies say 5G's promised speeds make game streaming while on the go possible. And a few tests,   including a recent one in South Korea, have proved promising.
 In the meantime, Apple's changed rules mean that the company is effectively reversing its earlier stance against allowing game streaming services on iPhones and iPads.
 Apple's   App Store Review Guidelines, which its employees use to decide whether programs submitted to the App Store meet the company's criteria around safety, usability and monetization, previously didn't allow game streaming services. In August, the company said in a statement that  it was concerned it couldn't review each individual game on a given game service. The company's changes make that possible.
 Microsoft, which previously criticized Apple's rules limiting its Xbox game streaming service, said in a statement that it disagrees with Apple's approach.
 "This remains a bad experience for customers," a Microsoft spokesman said in a statement. "Gamers want to jump directly into a game from their curated catalog within one app just like they do with movies or songs, and not be forced to download over 100 apps to play individual games from the cloud. We're committed to putting gamers at the center of everything we do, and providing a great experience is core to that mission."
   Game streaming services first made waves in 2010, when services like OnLive and Gaikai proved they could stream a game over the internet. Both companies were eventually bought by Sony, which started its PlayStation Now service in 2014. It's since   grown to offer more than 800 titles.
 Most recently, Microsoft, Google and chipmaker Nvidia have offered their takes on the technology, powered by subscriptions or individual game purchases or some combination of the two.
 Microsoft, for example, plans to offer streaming technology   through its $14.99 per month Xbox Games Pass Ultimate service, which gives people access to more than 100 games on the Xbox or PC, as well as its Xbox Live gaming social network. The company already debuted early access to the service for Android devices, but in August it said Apple's App Store rules meant the Xbox streaming service   couldn't run on iPhones or iPads.
 "Apple stands alone as the only general purpose platform to deny consumers cloud  gaming and game subscription services like Xbox Game Pass," the company said at the time. It's unclear whether Microsoft will ultimately concede to Apple's policy.
 Google and Nvidia declined to comment. Sony didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

title: 微软表示，苹果App Store的变化仍然给游戏玩家带来了糟糕的体验，他们想要一个目录应用程序，而不是“被迫下载100多个应用程序”
title_en: Microsoft says Apple's App Store changes still create a bad experience for gamers, who want a catalog app and not to “be forced to download over 100 apps”

在我职业生涯的早期，在多伦多的一家咨询公司进行了一次简短的初步面试后，我在同一天被邀请参加一个技术面试。我将加入的团队中的两名资深开发人员将进行面试。
采访开始时，他们愉快地描述了他们一直在做的项目：大学教授与学生交流的门户。它是用ASP.NETMVC构建的，这是我已经使用了几年的一个框架。我表示我对这个框架很满意，我很高兴能参与到这个项目中来。然后技术考试开始了，当考试结束时，我感到羞辱地离开了面试。
许多年后，当我准备接受采访时，当我回顾这段经历时，我意识到提问和接近的路线对那些开发人员来说是一次自我旅行。我向自己保证，永远不会让我的任何候选人有和我一样的感觉。
那些开发人员做错了什么？撇开他们对我的态度不谈，他们的问题与角色或项目无关。我当时不知道什么是红黑树，但我绝对知道如何使用ASP.NETMVC，他们没有询问过。
我对技术面试的金科玉律是，每一步、每一次谈话和每一个问题都必须与该职位的日常工作相关。虽然这一点可能是显而易见的，但我相信许多招聘经理仍然期待应聘者在参加技术面试时背诵计算机科学书籍。这种形式的技术面试应该被淘汰。
以我的黄金法则为指导，我的面试过程要简单得多。在面试之前，我和我的团队回顾了应聘者与我们分享(或在Github上写的)的代码样本，以了解他们的代码质量。在面试期间，我深入到了与应聘者讨论的三个领域：产品建设、过程坚持和团队合作。
回顾他们过去的经验，询问他们开发了哪些技术和产品，以及如何开发。我询问他们以前从概念发货到市场的产品。
为了了解他们派生解决方案的思维过程，我绘制了一个UI，并要求他们概述和解释他们将使用哪些方法、结构和技术来构建解决方案。
我问他们如何跟上技术的步伐，以及他们如何提高技能来衡量他们对工作的热情。
我回顾了他们的经验，询问他们是如何管理产品构建的，以及他们使用了哪些工具和流程。
我解释了在我们团队中工作的过程，并询问他们将如何改变这一过程，以及他们在哪里看到了效率低下的地方来讨论他们的想法。
通常，我会给他们一个场景，流程无法让团队发现他们将采取什么措施来解决效率低下的问题，以及他们是否愿意直言不讳。
在回顾他们过去的经历时，我问他们是如何与他们的团队合作和沟通的。
我提出了一个场景，其中他们可能缺乏某一领域的知识，并评估他们是否以及如何利用并与他们的团队协作。
我询问的另一个场景是，在评估他们如何管理冲突并将重点放在为团队交付结果方面，他们与团队成员存在分歧。
我在一次面试中这样做是为了记住应聘者的时间和我的时间。我想招聘应聘者，因为他们愿意学习、成长并挑战现状。
技术前景是这样的，一旦我们拥有了一套基线编程技能。那么，我们需要的是愿意挑战自己并保持开放的心态，因为每个开发人员几乎所有时间都必须在工作中学习。有鉴于此，技术面试是神秘的、学术性的，简直就是死路一条。
note_en:
Early in my career, after a short initial interview at a consulting firm in Toronto, I was invited to a technical interview on the same day. Two of the senior developers from the team I would join would conduct the interview.
 The interview started pleasantly with them describing the project they have been working on: a portal for university professors to communicate with their students. It was being built with ASP.NET MVC, a framework I had been working with for several years. I expressed that I was comfortable with the framework and I would be excited to work on the project. Then the technical examination began and on its conclusion I left the interview feeling humiliated.
 Many years later, when I was preparing to take an interview, I looked back on this experience to realize that the line of questioning and approach had been an ego trip for those developers. I promised myself to  never make any of my candidates feel the way I did.
 What did those developers do wrong? Leaving aside their attitude towards me, their questions had no relevance to the role or the project. I did not know what a red-black tree was at the time but I definitely knew how to use ASP.NET MVC which they did not inquire about.
 My golden rule for technical interviews is that, "Every step, conversation and question  must be pertinent to the day-to-day of the role." While this may be obvious, I am sure that many hiring managers are still expecting candidates to arrive at technical interviews with Computer Science books memorized. This form of technical interviews should be made obsolete.
  With my golden rule as guide, I conduct a much simpler interview process. Prior to an interview, my team and I review samples of code that the candidate shared with us (or had written on Github) to understand the quality of their code. And during the interview, I dive into three areas of discussions with the candidates: product building, process adherence and team work.
   Going through their past experience and asking about what technologies and products they built and how. I ask about previous products they have shipped from concept to market.
 To understand their thought process for deriving solutions, I draw an UI and ask them to outline and explain what approaches, structures and technologies they would use to build it out.
 I ask about how they keep up with technologies and how they improve their skills to gauge their passion for the work.
   I go over their experiences and ask about how they managed their product-building and what tools and processes they used.
 I explain the process of working on our team and ask how they would change it and where they see inefficiencies to discuss their thinking.
 Often, I will give them a scenario where the processes are failing the team to find what they would do to tackle inefficiencies and if they would be willing to speak up.
   While going through their past experiences, I ask about how they collaborated and communicated with their teams.
 I present a scenario where their knowledge in an area may be lacking and evaluate if and how they would leverage and collaborate with their team.
 Another scenario I ask about is where they disagree with their team-members to evaluate how they manage conflict and focus on delivering results for the team.
 I do this within one interview to be mindful of the candidate's time and mine. I want to hire candidates for their will to learn, grow and challenge the status quo.
 The technology landscape is such that once we have a set of baseline programming skills. What is needed then, is a willingness to challenge ourselves and stay open-minded because every developer will have to learn on the job almost all of the time. Given this, the technical interview is arcane, academic and as good as dead.
title: 技术面试是一次自我旅行
title_en: The Technical Interview Is an Ego Trip

我删除这个博客已经两个半月了，所以我欠你们所有人最近发生的事情的最新消息。
不管怎样，我还没有听到“纽约时报”的任何消息。因为什么都没有发表，我猜他们放弃了这篇文章，除非他们上个月联系了一位熟人进行了另一次采访。总体而言，我很困惑。
但是他们绝对没有给我任何明确的保证，他们不会泄露我的私人信息。现在我已经公开承认隐私对我很重要-出于这个原因，我以前试图避免过于强烈地谈论这一点-一些人自作主张在Twitter上到处张贴我的真名，以便骚扰和威胁我。在公开抗议“纽约时报”的行为时，我可能无意中使自己受到了史翠珊效应的影响；我仍然认为这样做是正确的。
在这一点上，我认为保持匿名是一场失败的战斗。因此，我正在逐步重新调整我的生活，以适应环境似乎迫使我受到的那种宣传。我和我的雇主进行了一次交谈，我们达成一致，我将逐步离开那里的工作。在某个时候，我可能会开始自己的私人诊所，在那里我是自己的老板，我可以专注于药物管理-而不是我最担心的那种心理治疗，这在道德上与作为公众人物不相容。我正试图最大限度地缓慢而谨慎地完成所有这些工作，而且不会给我的任何病人带来不必要的负担，这需要很长时间才能弄清楚。
我也在和Substack商量转向他们的博客平台。虽然我有一部分想回到这里来写博客，假装什么都没发生过，但Substack选项已经对我产生了吸引力。我想，作为一个专门承诺在需要时保护博客作者的大团体的一员，我会感到更安全。此外，有很多不同的收入来源，我会感到更安全，Substack给了我一个非常慷慨的报价。很多人就如何在没有Substack的情况下将我的博客货币化给了我很好的建议-我非常认真地对待这些建议，在不违反保密协议的情况下，我只能回答Substack的出价非常慷慨。
当我最初询问读者这种可能性时，他们提出了许多合理的担忧：他们中的一些人对Substack的评论系统感到困惑，另一些人对其弹出的订阅提醒感到恼火，其他人担心被困在付费墙外。我已经和Substack谈过这件事，他们做出了一些非常令人印象深刻的承诺来解决这些问题-他们将编码一个最大限度的SSC式的评论体验，他们将允许我选择退出订阅提醒，除了一些隐藏的开放线程，我不需要“付费”任何东西。现在不是我复习Substack要解决的几十个问题的示例的时候，但是假设我有您做过的大多数相同的问题，并投入了大量的工作来解决它们。
(如果你担心隐藏的开放线程，请查看Data Secrets Lox，这个论坛在过去几个月里很好地保持了SSC开放线程的传统。)。
这就是我现在所在的地方--试着让我的日常工作收尾，非常初步地规划一个私人诊所，并与Substack协商编写细节。我还在调查其他一些事情，以保护我的身体安全。当所有这些都完成后，我将重新开始写博客。现在我预计那是在10月到1月之间的某个时候-很明显，当它发生的时候，我会让你知道的。在此之前，如果人们继续尊重我对匿名的偏好，我将不胜感激。在那之后，我将不再那么在意-尽管我仍然会用“斯科特·亚历山大”来保持品牌不变，我仍然会尽我所能避免宣传。
我可能已经暗示了这一点，但我应该明确地说-我真的很感激在整个事件过程中得到的所有支持。你们这些人都很棒。稍后我会更详细地说明这一点，并更多地讨论一些具体的例子，但现在只需相信你们都很棒就行了。
我还是打算参加书评大赛！我会在开通新博客之后再做的！你们中那些给我发评论的人没有浪费你们的时间！这会发生的！瘟疫可能会折磨世界的每一个角落，天空可能会变红，太阳会在中午变黑，地球可能会摇晃，蝗灾可能会覆盖大地，但千万不要怀疑，在这一切都结束的那一天，在金色的未来，会有一场书评比赛。
正如这些天所有的孩子都在说的那样，“感谢你们在这些困难时期继续给予我们的支持”。
note_en:
It’s been two and a half months since I deleted the blog, so I owe all of you an update on  recent events.
 I haven’t heard anything from the  New York Times one way or the other. Since nothing has been published, I’d assume they dropped the article, except that they approached an acquaintance for another interview last month. Overall I’m confused.
 But they definitely haven’t given me any explicit reassurance that they won’t reveal my private information. And now that I’ve publicly admitted privacy is important to me – something I tried to avoid coming on too strong about before, for exactly this reason – some people have taken it upon themselves to post my real name all over Twitter in order to harass and threaten me. In publicly protesting the NYT’s actions I probably inadvertantly Streisand-Effect-ed myself; I still think it was the right thing to do.
 At this point I think maintaining anonymity is a losing battle. So I am gradually reworking my life to be compatible with the sort of publicity that circumstances seem to be forcing on me. I had a talk with my employer and we came to a mutual agreement that I would gradually transition away from working there. At some point, I may start my own private practice, where I’m my own boss and where I can focus on medication management – and not the kinds of psychotherapy that I’m most worried are ethically incompatible with being a public figure. I’m trying to do all of this maximally slowly and carefully and in a way that won’t cause undue burden to any of my patients, and it’s taking a long time to figure out.
 I’m also talking to  Substack about moving to their blogging platform. While part of me wants to jump right back into blogging here and pretend nothing ever happened, the Substack option has grown on me. I think I’d feel safer as part of a big group that specifically  promises to defend their bloggers when needed. And also, I’d feel safer with a lot of diverse income streams, and Substack has made me an extremely generous offer. Many people gave me good advice about how I could monetize my blog without Substack – I took these suggestions very seriously, and without violating a confidentiality agreement all I can answer is that Substack’s offer was  extremely generous.
 When I  originally asked readers about this possibility, they raised a lot of valid concerns: some of them were confused by Substack’s commenting system, others annoyed by its pop-up reminders to subscribe, others were concerned about being stuck outside a paywall. I’ve talked to Substack about this, and they’ve made some really impressive promises to address these things – they’re going to code a maximally-SSC-like commenting experience, they’re going to let me opt out of the subscription reminders, I won’t have to “paywall” anything besides some Hidden Open Threads. This isn’t the time for me to go over the dozens of examples of concerns I had that Substack went above and beyond to address, but assume I had most of the same ones you did and put a lot of work into addressing them.
 (and if you’re worried about the Hidden Open Threads, check out  Data Secrets Lox, a forum that has done a great job keeping the SSC Open Thread tradition going over the past few months.)
 So that’s where I am right now – trying to wind things down at my day job, very preliminarily planning  a private practice, and negotiating writing details with Substack. I’m also looking into some other things to protect my physical safety. When all of that is done, I’ll start blogging again. Right now I’m expecting that to be some time between October and January – and obviously when it happens I’ll let you know. I would appreciate if people continued to respect my preferences about anonymity until then. After that I’ll stop caring as much – though I’ll still go by “Scott Alexander” to keep the brand the same, and I’ll still do what I can to avoid publicity.
 I might have hinted at this already, but I should say it explicitly – I’m really grateful for all the support I got throughout this whole incident. You people are all great. I’ll say so at more length later, and talk more about some specific examples, but for now just accept on faith that you’re all great.
 I still plan to do the book review contest! I’ll do it sometime after I start the new blog! Those of you who sent me reviews didn’t waste your time! It’s going to happen! Pestilence may afflict every corner of the world,  the skies may turn red as blood and the sun go dark at noon, the  earth may shake and  plagues of locusts cover the land, but never doubt that there will be a book review contest someday, in the golden future, when all of this is over.
 As all the kids are saying these days, “thank you for your continuing support during these difficult times”.
title: 我的最新情况

note:
微软现在正在测试将“程序和功能”控制面板重定向到现代的“应用程序和功能”设置页面，从而继续削弱令人尊敬的Windows10控制面板。
当微软发布Windows8时，给用户留下的是既有传统控制面板选项又有新的现代设置部分的令人困惑的混合。
直到今天，Windows10用户仍然需要同时使用控制面板和设置界面来管理不同的操作系统设置。
在过去的一年里，Microsoft已经开始开发新的现代设置页面，以取代现有的控制面板和Microsoft管理控制台(MMC)设置和应用程序。
这些新的设置页面包括用于更换系统控制面板的更新的关于页面和全新的磁盘管理设置工具。
BedepingComputer认为，这样做是为了最终过渡到完全现代化的设置功能，并最终完全取消控制面板。
随着昨天发布的Windows10预览版20211的发布，微软研究员拉斐尔·里维拉发现了一个隐藏的功能，它再次削弱了传统的控制面板。
通常，当您进入Windows 10控制面板并单击程序和功能时，会显示一个旧版控制面板，您可以在其中卸载或更改程序的安装选项。
启用Rivera发现的隐藏功能后，当您尝试访问程序和功能控制面板时，它会将用户重定向到最新的应用程序和功能设置页面，如下所示。
此功能是Microsoft将用户缓慢迁移到较新的设置界面，同时慢慢减少控制面板的一种方式。
如果您希望现在测试此重定向，您可以加入Windows Insider程序并安装Windows10预览版20211或更高版本。
要获得此版本，您需要加入Windows Insider计划的开发人员频道。
安装最新的Windows 10内部版本后，请按照以下步骤启用Windows 10中的隐藏功能：
下载ViveTool，它用于在Windows 10中启用隐藏的开发人员功能。下载后，解压zip文件。
启用隐藏功能后，您可以关闭命令提示符，重定向将自动工作，而无需重新启动Windows。
测试完成后，您可以通过从提升的命令提示符运行以下ViveTool命令来禁用此功能。
删除配置后，您将可以再次访问标准程序和功能控制面板。
note_en:
Microsoft continues to chip away at the venerable Windows 10 Control Panel by now testing the redirection of the 'Program and Features' control panel to the modern 'Apps & Features' Settings page.
 When Microsoft released Windows 8, users were left with a confusing mix of legacy Control Panel options and a new modern Settings section.
 To this day, Windows 10 users are still required to use both the Control Panel and Settings interface to manage different operating system settings.
 Over the past year, Microsoft has begun developing new modern Settings pages that replace existing Control Panel and Microsoft Management Console (MMC) settings and applications.
 These new Settings pages include  an updated About page to replace the System control panel and a  modern disk management Settings tool.
  BleepingComputer believes this is being done to transition to a completely modern Settings feature eventually and ultimately do away with the Control Panel altogether.
  With the release of yesterday's Windows 10 preview build 20211, Microsoft researcher  Rafael Rivera discovered a hidden feature that once again chips away at the legacy Control Panel.
 Usually, when you go into the Windows 10 Control Panel and click on 'Programs and Features,' you are shown a legacy control panel where you can uninstall or change installation options for programs.
  When the hidden feature discovered by Rivera is enabled, it will redirect users to the modern 'Apps & Features' Settings page when you try to access the 'Program and Features' control panel, as demonstrated below.
  This feature is a way for Microsoft to slowly migrate users to the newer Settings interface while slowly chipping away at the Control Panel.
  If you wish to test this redirect now, you can join the Windows Insider programs and install Windows 10 preview build 20211 or later.
 To get this build, you will need to join the 'Dev' channel of the Windows Insider program.
 Once the latest Windows 10 insider build is installed, please follow these steps to enable the hidden feature in Windows 10:
 Download ViveTool, which is used to enable hidden developer features in Windows 10. Once downloaded, extract the zip file.
   After enabling the hidden feature, you can close the Command Prompt, and the redirect will automatically work without restarting Windows.
 When done testing, you can disable this feature by running the following ViveTool command from an elevated command prompt.
  Once you delete the configuration, you will be able to access the standard Programs and Features control panel again.
title: Windows10控制面板的棺材上又钉上了一颗钉子
title_en: Windows 10 Control Panel gets another nail in its coffin

note:
ProPublica是一个调查滥用权力的非营利性新闻编辑室。注册后即可收到我们最重要的新闻。
美国平等就业机会委员会发布了一项全面的决定，得出结论认为，IBM在2013年至2018年期间参与了系统性的年龄歧视，当时该公司在美国解雇了数千名年长的员工。
平等就业机会委员会的调查结果包含在8月31日写给一群前员工的一封信中。两年多前，ProPublica报道称，该公司经常藐视或绕过旨在保护老年员工免受招聘和解雇偏见的法律。
这封信说，平等就业机会委员会在全国范围内进行的一项调查“发现了(IBM)最高层自上而下的信息，指示经理们采取积极措施，大幅减少老年员工的人数，为年轻员工腾出空间”。
劳动法专家表示，该机构的调查结果可能导致IBM面临数百万美元的和解款项或联邦诉讼，而此时该公司正面临持续的竞争压力，且正在进行管理层换届。
note_en:
ProPublica is a nonprofit newsroom that investigates abuses of power. Sign up to receive  our biggest stories as soon as they’re published.
  The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has issued a sweeping decision concluding that IBM engaged in systematic age discrimination between 2013 and 2018, when it shed thousands of older workers in the United States.
  The EEOC finding, contained in an Aug. 31  letter to a group of ex-employees, comes more than two years after  ProPublica reported that the company regularly flouted or outflanked laws intended to protect older workers from bias in hiring and firing.
  The letter says a nationwide EEOC investigation “uncovered top-down messaging from (IBM’s) highest ranks directing managers to engage in an aggressive approach to significantly reduce the headcount of older workers to make room for” younger ones.
  Employment law experts said the agency’s finding could result in IBM facing millions of dollars in settlement payments or a federal lawsuit at a time when the company is under continued competitive pressure and in the midst of a management changeover.
title: 美国平等就业机会委员会确认IBM存在年龄歧视模式
title_en: The U.S. EEOC Confirms a Pattern of Age Discrimination at IBM

note:
当一段关系开始于不诚实或被操纵的行为时，这将是一段有毒的关系。
大多数主要的虚拟主机公司都以操控的方式培养潜在客户，但不幸的是，由于天真或需要，许多公司没有意识到麻烦的迹象。
这里有一个重要但常见的提示，即你的未来网站主持人不会尊重你，你将陷入一段妥协的、功能失调的关系中。
在上面的广告中，这位普通的年轻女性刚刚接触到了可能是网络主持人中的王子-迷人的蓝主持人本人。能以每月3.5美元的价格拥有他，她感到既兴奋又受宠若惊。
现在这位年轻的女士很兴奋，因为虽然这看起来太好了，简直不像是真的，但看起来这可能就是真爱。一个月3.95美元！事情终于开始好转了。
在对她的域名进行了一番轻浮的初步讨论后，我们的白马王子蓝星终于谈到了他的意图。
现在我们很快就谈到了“把你的手从我身上拿开！”这段关系。
有人可能会争辩说，如果你把这些数字分成3年，就等于所说的价格：3.95美元。
如果每月3美元95美元，我可以付第一个月3美元95美元，第二个…3美元95美元。第三款…售价3美元9角5分。等。
所以按照这个逻辑，结婚50年后，一枚1000美元的结婚戒指每月要花1.66美元。假设没有离婚的话。
可悲的是，一些人会忽视这段有毒关系的第一个警报信号，并说服自己，他们不仅仅是被操纵了。
可悲的是，这笔“交易”在很短的时间内就失去了，因为像这样的房东固有的普通追加销售。因为如果你不说“把你的手从我身上拿开”，你就会引发未来的攻击，而网络主机知道这一点。他很擅长他的工作。
尽管这位白马王子号称是多么独特和有价值，但每条胡同里都有一条满是白马王子的街道。
不要放弃你的标准，安顿下来。好的东道主很难找到，因为他们不会在社会/互联网的主要交通区域闲逛。
note_en:
When a relationship starts with an act of dishonesty or manipulation, it will be a toxic relationship.
  Most major web hosting companies groom potential customers in manipulative ways, but, sadly, many do not recognize the trouble signs due to naivety or need.
 Here is one important, but common cue that your webhost-to-be will not respect you, and you are in for a compromising, dysfunctional relationship.
     In the ad above, this average young woman has just been approached by what could be the prince-charming of webhosts, bluehost himself. She is excited and flattered that she could have him for the price of $3.50 a month.
     Now the young lady is excited, because although it seemed too good to be true, it looks like this might just be true love. $3.95 a month! Things are finally looking up.
  After some flirty initial conversation of what her domain name will be, our prince charming bluehost finally gets down to his intentions.
    And now we’ve quickly arrived at that part of the relationship known as   “Get your hands off me!".
   One might argue that if you divide the numbers over 3 years, it evens out to the price stated: $3.95.
   If it was $3.95 a month, I could pay $3.95 for the first month, $3.95 for the second… $3.95 for the third … etc.
   So by this logic, a $1000 wedding ring costs $1.66 dollars a month after 50 years of marriage. Assuming there is no divorce.
  Sadly, some people will ignore the first alarm signs of the toxic relationship, and convince themselves that they did not just get manipulated.
 Sadly, that “deal” is lost in a short time due to common upsells inherent to hosts such as this. Because if you don’t say “get your hands off me” you enable future exploits, and the web host knows this. He’s good at what he does.
  Despite how unique and valuable this prince charming purports to be, there is a street full of them coming from every alley way.
  Don’t give up your standards and settle. The good hosts are hard to find, because they don’t hang out in the main traffic areas of society/internet.
title: 你的网络主机不会尊重你的第一个主要迹象
title_en: The first major sign your web host will not respect you

note:
那个面目可憎的老魔术师这一次不见了；我现在是主人，我是战术家，他所有的鬼魂都必须听从我的命令。也知道他的咒语、咒语和手势；凭着我的头脑创造的奇迹，我应该这样做。-约翰·沃尔夫冈·冯·歌德(魔法师的学徒)。
学者们一边仔细研读旧论文，一边钻研他们的电脑，对他们的研究主题有了深入的了解。当他们在想法空间中规划自己的道路时，他们会发展出一种深刻的直觉，哪些技术在实践中效果很好。不幸的是，这些来之不易的见解中有许多没有发表，仍然模糊不清。
去年，我在奥地利林茨的约翰尼斯·开普勒大学(Johannes Kepler University)上了一门课程，主题是递归神经网络和长短期记忆网络。在那里，赛普·霍克赖特(Sepp Hochreiter)分享了他和他的团队在培训LSTM时使用的一些“魔术”。这篇博文是我一些笔记的积累。
对于这篇文章，我假设您已经熟悉了LSTM。如果没有，我建议您从Chris Olah对LSTM网络的理解开始，然后继续阅读LSTM的原著(Hochreiter和Schmidhuber，1997)。
在我们开始之前，我还想强调一些在动机上有相似之处的资源：
LSTM：搜索空间奥德赛(Greff等人)。2017)来自IDSIA(戴尔摩尔人工智能研究所)。
如果您想要试验一些介绍的技术，并且需要一个灵活的基于Pytorch的LSTM实现，我推荐Michael Widrich的LSTM工具库。
所展示的技术的所有功劳都归功于作者。他们陈述中的所有错误都是我的。我总是渴望得到反馈。
Vanilla LSTM是最流行的变体之一，通常是流行软件库中的默认LSTM架构。它的特点是有三个门和一个存储状态-这些门为模型提供容量，保护存储单元不受信息和噪声的干扰；它们使LSTM的动态高度非线性，并允许它学习执行复杂的操作。
让我们简要介绍一下Vanilla LSTM的机制，以介绍本文中使用的符号和术语。在给定时间步长流入LSTM细胞的感觉输入\(\boldSymbol{x}(T)\)被转换为细胞输入激活\(\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)-\(\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)的元素由非线性函数\(g(\cdot)\)激活，该函数在实践中通常被定义为双曲正切或tanh。通过按元素将\(\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)乘以S型激活的输入门\(\boldSymbol{i}(T)\)，删除与当前时间步长无关的信息。类似地，使用S型激活的遗忘门\(\boldSymbol{f}(T)\)部分擦除前一时间点\(\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)\)的单元状态。新存储单元状态\(\boldSymbol{c}(T)\)是通过将当前单元状态更新\(\boldSymbol{i}(T)\ODOT\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)添加到过滤后的旧状态\(\boldSymbol{f}(T)\ODOT\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)\)来计算的。最后，LSTM使用存储单元激活函数\(h(\cdot)\)将存储内容压缩到特定的数字范围内，并通过输出门\(\boldSymbol{o}(T)\)过滤结果。这导致最终存储单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(T)\)。
\[\BEGIN{ALIGN}\boldSymbol{i}(T)&amp；=\sigma\Left(\boldSymbol{W}_{i}^{\top}\boldSymbol{x}(T)+\boldSymbol{R}_{i}^{\top}\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\右)\boldSymbol{o}(T)&amp；=\sigma\Left(\boldbol{W}_{o}^{\top}\boldbol{x}(T)+\boldbol{R}_{o}^{\top}\boldbol{y}(t-1)\right)\boldbol{f}(T)&amp；=\sigma\Left(\boldSymbol{W}_{f}^{\top}\boldSymbol{x}(T)+\boldSymbol{R}_{f}^{\top}\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\right)\boldSymbol{z}(T)&amp；=g\Left(\boldSymbol{W}_{z}^{\top}\boldSymbol{x}(T)+\boldSymbol{R}_{z}^{\top}\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\right)\boldSymbol{c}(T)&amp；=\boldSymbol{f}(T)\ODOT\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)+\boldSymbol{i}(T)\ODOT\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)+\boldSymbol{i}(T)\ODOT\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)+\boldSymbol{i}(T)。=\boldSymbol{o}(T)\odot h(\boldSymbol{c}(T))\end{align}\]。
其中\(\sigma\)表示Sigmoid激活函数，\(\ODOT\)表示元素或Hadamard乘积。请注意，每个门都可以访问当前输入\(\boldSymbol{x}(T)\)和先前的单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\)。
还要记住，权重\(W\)和重复权\(R\)在时间步长之间是共享的。
在实践中，通常选择输入激活函数\(g\)为tanh。但这一选择并不明显，事实上，在最初的LSTM文件中，Sigmoid被用来激活\(\boldSymbol{z}\)。由于存储单元的目的是随时间学习和记忆模式，因此S形激活是指示输入中实体的存在(激活值接近\(1\))或不存在(激活值接近\(0\))的自然选择。另一方面，Tanh的下限为\(-1\)似乎没有直观的意义。什么是否定模式？值为\(-1\)的激活是否表示输入中不存在某些内容？
采用TANH首先需要两次精神上的转变。首先，Tanh在元学习环境中具有直觉意义。我们现在用记忆单元来存储另一个神经网络的权重，而不是模式。要指示权重值是应该增加还是应该减少，我们需要正值和负值。
第二个直观的解释是暗示的存储。在此上下文中的暗示是支持或反对某事的证据。考虑一个来自文本分析的例子。假设一个模型在一个段落中遇到单词“团队”、“球员”和“目标”。这些都是强烈暗示正文是关于体育的，但如果下一段包括“经理人”、“收入”和“股东”等词，就是一个强烈的迹象，表明该段实际上描述了一个商业背景。正值可以被视为有利于某些类别的暗示，负值可以被视为不利于某些类别的暗示。
但是，有一个简单的数学原因，为什么tanh是激活单元格输入的首选。与常用的激活函数(如RELU和Sigmoid)相比，单元输入激活的期望值对于tanh为零(在零均值高斯预激活的假设下)：
要了解为什么需要此属性，请记住单元格状态是如何在给定时间步骤1更新的：
在每个时间步，我们将单元格输入激活\(\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)(由输入门过滤)添加到前一个单元格状态\(\boldSymbol{c}(t-1)\)。如果我们为\(\boldSymbol{z}\)选择一个激活函数，使得每个激活都有一个值\(\geq 0\)(例如，Sigmoid、RELU等)，则\(\boldSymbol{c}\)将很快具有非常大的值。即使激活相对较小，对于足够长的序列，细胞状态也会变大。这个问题被称为漂移效应。
但是，大的记忆细胞状态怎么会成为学习的障碍呢？要回答这个问题，我们需要看看LSTM的回传：
\[\BEGIN{ALIGNED}\FRAC{\PARTIAL L}{\PARTIAL\boldSymbol{c}(T)}}&amp；=\frac{\Partial L}{\Partial\boldSymbol{y}(T)}\color{#9900ff}{\FRAC{\Partial\boldSymbol{y}(T)}{\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(T)}}+\frac{\Partial L}{\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(t+1)}\FRAC{\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(t+1)}{\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(t。=\frac{\Partial L}{\Partial\boldSymbol{y}(T)}\OperatorName{diag}\Left(\boldSymbol{o}(T)\odot\color{#9900ff}{h^{\prime}(\boldsymbol{c}(t))}\right)+\frac{\partial L}{\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(t+1)}\End{Alignment}\]。
该方程递归地汇总来自未来的所有误差信号，并在时间上向后携带它们。直观地描述了时间t的细胞状态对损耗L的不同影响方式。我们仔细看一下突出显示的术语\(\Partial\boldSymbol{y}(T)/\Partial\boldSymbol{c}(T)\)，它描述了存储单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(T)\)如何随着\(\boldSymbol{c}(T)\)的改变而改变。可以通过计算\(\\运算符名{diag}\Left(\boldSymbol{o}(T)\odoth^{\Prime}(\boldSymbol{c}(T))\Right)\)来获得偏导数。现在我们有麻烦了。请记住，我们使用tanh作为存储单元激活函数\(h\)，以将存储单元状态压缩到数值范围\((-1，1)\)。其导数定义如下：
现在，如果由于漂移效应\(\boldSymbol{c}(T)\)变得非常大，则对于\(\boldSymbol{c}(T)\)中的每个元素，第二个等式中突出显示的项将计算为\(h^{\Prime}(\boldSymbol{c}(T))=1-1=0\)。结果，\(FRAC{\PARTIAL L}{\PARTIAL y(T)}\FRAC{\PARTIAL Y(T)}{\PARTIAL C(T)}\)将为零，并且单元状态失去其影响存储单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(T)\)的能力，并因此损失\(L\)。
我们可以看到，选择tanh作为我们的输入激活函数\(g\)要优于其他常用的函数，因为它消除了漂移效应。由于其范围集中在零附近，因此可以防止单元状态随时间累积。这反过来又稳定了学习信号，这是学习长期依赖的关键。
为了防止细胞状态积累，Felix Gehrs和Jürgen Schmidhuber提出了遗忘门(Gers，Schmidhuber和Cummins，1999)。其想法是允许网络在每次更新之前学习擦除小区状态。
然而，遗忘门的主要问题是它可能会重新引入消失的梯度问题-矛盾的是，LSTM架构的构建首先就是为了消除这个问题。让我们来看看忘记门是如何影响渐变的：
如果没有遗忘门，梯度等于单位矩阵。消除了渐变消失问题：
尽管遗忘门的使用可能有争议，但在训练序列很短的情况下，忘记门通常工作得非常好。为了减少有问题的梯度的影响，建议用较大的正值初始化遗忘门的偏置单元。这使得栅极最初是打开的(激活接近1)，并将梯度推向单位矩阵。
遗忘门的一种常见变体是将其绑定到输入门。旧信息从小区状态被擦除到与允许新信息流入的程度相同的程度。结果是简化了架构，减少了可训练参数2。小区状态更新如下：
聚焦LSTM是一种简化的LSTM变体，没有忘记门。它的主要动机是将单元输入激活\(\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)和门之间的关注点分离。在Vanilla LSTM中，\(\boldSymbol{z}\)和门都取决于当前外部输入\(\boldSymbol{x}(T)\)和先前的存储单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\)。这可能会导致\(\boldSymbol{i}\)和\(\boldSymbol{z}\)之间的冗余。对于要被激活的\(\boldSymbol{i}(T)\odt\boldSymbol{z}(T)\)，\(\boldSymbol{i}\)和\(\boldSymbol{z}\)都需要激活，并且因为两者都访问相同的信息，所以它们通常学习相似的权重(即，当它们检测到相同的图案时)，或者它们中的任何一个收敛到常数值3。
对于聚焦的LSTM，\(\boldSymbol{z}\)仅看到当前输入\(\boldSymbol{x}(T)\)，而门的决定完全基于先前的存储单元状态激活\(\boldSymbol{y}(t-1)\)。首先，\(\boldSymbol{z}\)检测输入序列中的模式，然后输入门根据内部状态缩放\(\boldSymbol{z}\)。
这种信息访问划分比普通的LSTM有一些优势。首先，将可训练参数的个数减少到\(2i^{2}+i_D\)，没有偏置单位-其中\(D\)和\(i\)分别是输入维数和隐维数。其次，新信息进入存储单元的唯一方式现在是通过\(\boldSymbol{z}\)。这使得对LSTM的动态进行分析和推理变得容易得多，因此有助于提高可解释性。
我们继续拆除香草LSTM的机器。通过从聚焦的LSTM中移除输出门，我们得到了轻量级LSTM体系结构：
我们为什么要这么做呢？同样，我们用网络的自由度换取了更少的参数、更高的速度和可解释性。另一个原因是冗余。在Vanilla/Focked LSTM中，信息首先由当前单元的输出门过滤，然后通过管道输送到下一个单元的输入门。我们可以通过允许输入门访问未过滤的信息并丢弃输出门来简化这两步过程。输出门的另一个可能的缺点是它可能导致振荡-输出门在交替的时间步骤4打开和关闭。
想象一下读一本书。我们不是线性阅读的。我们跳来跳去，读完一段难懂的文章，偶尔会停下来沉思。Recurn Networks不能做到这一点。他们没有一种内部机制允许他们暂停并仔细考虑，例如，序列中的某个标记。RNN在每个时间步长花费相等的计算量。
用算法模拟思考的一种简单方法是在给定的时间步重复向网络馈送先前的输入或中性元素(例如，零张量)-我们让输入自动收报机特定次数。这些额外的自动收报机或思考步骤背后的直觉是提高网络的抽象级别。递归神经网络可以被视为前馈神经网络，其中层数与输入序列的长度相同，并且各层之间共享权重。通过重复某些元素，我们可以创建更深、更具表现力的网络，其深度超出了输入序列自然定义的深度。
然而，这个概念带来了一些问题。网络是否应该在这些深思熟虑的步骤中发出输出？我们应该让输入自动收报机走几步？应该重复哪些时间步骤？
Alex Graves(Graves 2016)的自适应计算时间算法解决了这些问题。所呈现的机制允许RNN了解在接收输入和发出输出之间要采取多少考虑步骤：
在每个时间步长，RNN使用以下方程式集计算中间状态序列\(\Left(s_{t}^{1}，\ldots，s_{t}^{N(T)}\right)\)和中间输出序列\(\Left(y_{t}^{1}，\ldots，y_{t}^{N(T)}\right)\)：
\[\begin{array}{l}s_{t}^{n}=\left\{\begin{array}{l}\mathcal{S}\left(s_{t-1}，x_{t}^{1}\Right)\Text{if}n=1\数学{S}\Left(s_{t}^{n-1}，x_{t}^{n}\Right)\Text{否则}\End{数组}\Right。\\y_{t}^{n}=W_{y}s_{t}^{n}+b_{y}\end{array}\]
但是，网络如何确定在任何给定的时间步长应该采取多少中间报价器步骤呢？为此，作者引入了由中间态\(s_{t}^{n}\)导出的附加S型停机单元：
在每个中间步骤之后，我们检查直到当前中间步骤的所有先前停止单元的总和是否大于或等于\(1-\ε\)，其中\(\ε\)是确保计算也可以在单个中间步骤之后停止的小的正常数，例如，ε\(0.1\)。在每个中间步骤之后，我们检查所有先前停止单元的总和是否大于或等于\(1-\ε\)，其中\(\ε\)是确保计算也可以在单个中间步骤之后停止的小的正常数。如果满足条件，则停止计算；如果不满足，则再添加一个中间步骤，并重复该过程。
一旦确定了中间步数\(N(T)\)，我们就可以产生当前时间步长的最终状态\(s_t\)和最终输出\(y_t\)。为此，我们首先使用停止单位来确定中间时间点的停止概率\(p_{t}^{n}\)：
最后，我们分别计算了停机概率与中间状态和输出之间的内积的状态和输出在\(t\)处的值：
我们现在已经看到了如何增强任何递归神经网络，以便它能够学习将计算资源自适应地分配到序列中的不同时间步，或者换句话说，是暂停和思考。
我们遇到的一个常见问题是，对于长序列，存储单元状态可能会变得非常大。我们已经看到了如何使用tanh作为输入激活函数，通过将预期中的内存增量集中在零附近来消除这种漂移效应。
另一种补充方法是使用负偏置单元初始化输入门-以及可选的单元输入激活\(\boldSymbol{z}\)。因此，门对大多数输入进行强过滤，网络应该学会只有在检测到相关模式时才打开门。我们应该为偏移单位选择哪些值？这是对记忆单元的小输入和足够大的梯度以稳定学习之间的权衡。固体偏置初始化在\((-1，-10)\)范围内，但根据任务的不同，该值可能会有所不同。
另一种有趣的偏置初始化策略是用负值递减序列初始化输入和输出门的偏置单元；例如，\(1-i)/2)_{i=1}^{i}\)，其中\(i\)枚举存储单元。此方案背后的直觉是，即使是非常简单的任务，LSTM通常也会使用其全部容量。这可能会在学习的后期阶段导致困难，因为网络的大部分内存容量已经因为冗余地存储简单的模式而耗尽。使用负级联来初始化偏置引入了存储单元之间的排序，并鼓励该模型用尽可能少的单位来解决该任务。这是因为具有较高(即较少负)偏置单元的存储单元单元在反向传递期间携带较强的学习信号。随着学习的进行，时间反向传播将一个接一个地“打开”剩余的单元。
该方案已成功应用于Sepp Hochreiter等人的同源性检测工作中。Al(Hochreiter，Heusel和Obermayer，2007)。
挤压函数\(g\)和\(h\)是促成LSTM单元内数值稳定环境的两个重要机制-将输入和单元状态都保持在固定的数值范围内。在输入序列中只有几个元素与任务相关的情况下，使用标量\(\alpha&gt；1\)(通常设置为\(4\))放大激活会很有帮助。
这是为什么？想象一下LSTM扫描一个输入序列。它检测一个模式，用\(g\)重新调整它的比例，通过将其与输入门相乘来过滤结果激活，然后将其存储。到目前为止，指示存在图案的信号趋于衰减。然后，当我们使用\(h\)重新调整存储单元状态时，信号可能不够强，无法激活单元输出。结果，存储的图案失去了影响其他单元激活的能力。当栅极打开时，定标\(g\)提供更强的信号-该信号强到足以使存储单元激活函数\(h\)有所不同。
缩放\(h\)具有类似的效果，并且可用于使存储单元状态中的微小变化可识别。
这里要说明的是，存储模式携带的信号应该被放大到足以使单元输出有所不同。
使用\(g\)的恒等式可以加快学习过程，因为激活函数的导数不会缩放学习信号。当然，主要缺点是单元状态增量可能很大，并且存储单元可能开始漂移。
线性存储单元状态激活函数\(h\)在存储单元用于计数或累积信息的情况下特别有用。
